message:
test(readme): extend MultiIndex coverage + expose MultiIndexOrIndex (fd90.201 / br-w8156)

Extends readme_multiindex_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover the
full MultiIndex API documented at README line 435 plus the DataFrame
integration at line 437. Previously the test exercised 3 of 8 methods
plus zero DataFrame integration calls.

New coverage:
- MultiIndex::from_arrays (level-major construction)
- mi.droplevel(level) returning MultiIndexOrIndex (matched both
  Multi and Index variants — variant name is `Multi`, not `MultiIndex`,
  caught during test write)
- mi.swaplevel(i, j)
- mi.reorder_levels(&[usize])
- df.set_index_multi(&[col1, col2], drop, sep) — DataFrame integration
- df.to_multi_index(&[col1, col2]) — extract standalone MultiIndex

Defect found and fixed: MultiIndexOrIndex was exposed at the top-level
re-export (line 33) but missing from the prelude module. README's
droplevel example returns this type — user code following the README
with `use frankenpandas::prelude::*` would not compile. Added to prelude
alongside MultiIndex.

Verified: 34 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -849,6 +849,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-w4uu","title":"[MEDIUM] parity: MultiIndex .loc multi-axis slicing semantics","description":"Parity-gap audit: MultiIndex `.loc` multi-axis slicing.

**Scope.** pandas `df.loc[(level_0_key, level_1_key), columns]` indexes a row-MultiIndex DataFrame with a tuple. Supports:
- Exact tuple match: `df.loc[('A', 1)]` → Series.
- Partial tuple: `df.loc['A']` → sub-DataFrame (only outer-level filter).
- Slice in tuple: `df.loc[(slice(None), 1)]` → cross-section on inner level.
- pd.IndexSlice builder: `df.loc[pd.IndexSlice[:, 1], :]`.
- Slicing: `df.loc['A':'B', :]` requires sorted MultiIndex; raises `KeyError` otherwise.

**FrankenPandas state.** fp-index exposes `MultiIndex` struct and `DataFrame::xs` / `xs_level`. Check:

```
grep -n "pub fn loc\b\|loc_multi\|xs\|IndexSlice" crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs
grep -n "pub fn " crates/fp-index/src/lib.rs | head -20
```

DISC-006 documents scaffolded row-MultiIndex status. This bead is the concrete audit of `.loc` tuple semantics.

**Severity.** MEDIUM — MultiIndex is claimed in the README capability table but .loc edge cases are not proven at parity.

**Fix shape.**
1. Live-oracle tests in crates/fp-conformance/src/tests/conformance_multiindex_loc.rs covering 10 inputs: exact tuple match, partial tuple (outer), partial tuple (inner via slice), sorted-range slice, unsorted-range slice (expect error), pd.IndexSlice form, missing key KeyError, duplicate MultiIndex labels, NaN level values, empty frame.
2. Any divergence → child [HIGH] bead per failure case.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T02:11:52.107787752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:55.891849103Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:55.889372396Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["loc","multiindex","parity-gap","review"]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-w8156","title":"fd90.201: Extend MultiIndex test to cover from_arrays/droplevel/swaplevel/reorder_levels + DF integration","description":"fd90.201: Extend MultiIndex integration test to cover full API surface at README line 435.

The README enumerates: from_tuples, from_arrays, from_product, get_level_values, droplevel, swaplevel, reorder_levels, to_flat_index. The existing test exercises 3 of these (from_product, get_level_values, to_flat_index); fd90.192 added from_tuples coverage. Missing: from_arrays, droplevel, swaplevel, reorder_levels.

Plus the DataFrame integration claims (line 437): set_index_multi, to_multi_index. Neither is integration-tested.

Scope:
- Extend readme_multiindex_operations_compiles_and_runs to add:
  - MultiIndex::from_arrays (level-major construction)
  - mi.droplevel(level) returning MultiIndexOrIndex
  - mi.swaplevel(i, j)
  - mi.reorder_levels(&[usize])
  - df.set_index_multi(&[col1, col2], drop, sep)
  - df.to_multi_index(&[col1, col2])

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (~30 lines added).
Time estimate: 25-35min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:51:09.323132176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:53:37.431866830Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:53:37.429789515Z","close_reason":"Extended readme_multiindex_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover the full MultiIndex API at README line 435 plus the DataFrame integration at line 437: from_arrays (level-major), droplevel (returns MultiIndexOrIndex with Multi/Index variants), swaplevel(0,1), reorder_levels(&[1,0]), df.set_index_multi(&[col1,col2], drop, sep), df.to_multi_index. Also added MultiIndexOrIndex to the prelude module (was top-level-only, but droplevel returns it so user code following the README couldn't compile without it). All 34 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-wa26","title":"fd90.68: fp-groupby needs crate-level rustdoc for groupby aggregation engine","description":"Continuation of fd90.61-67 doc audit. Add crate-level //! rustdoc to fp-groupby - the standalone groupby aggregation engine. Public surface: GroupByOptions / GroupByExecutionOptions, GroupByError, AggFunc enum, groupby_sum / mean / count / min / max / first / last / std / var / median / nunique / prod / size aggregations, groupby_agg dispatcher, HyperLogLog approximate-distinct-count primitive. Pure docs. Verify cargo doc cleanly under RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:20:27.880362095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:22:33.296973084Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:22:33.294410403Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-we6ql","title":"fd90.146: lock in README Quick Example via integration test (regression guard for prelude)","description":"fd90.74-145 README audit. The fd90.121-fd90.144 sequence expanded the frankenpandas prelude to cover the README's documented API surface. The 6 unit tests inside src/lib.rs (prelude_smoke_test, prelude_concat_works, prelude_query_works, prelude_io_roundtrip, prelude_to_datetime_works, rusqlite_reexport_quickstart_compiles) exercise individual prelude items but not the END-TO-END Quick Example as a single script.

Without a single integration test that runs the README's Quick Example top-to-bottom, future prelude changes (or accidental removals) wouldn't surface a regression until users complain.

Fix: add crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (an integration test, NOT in src/lib.rs) that mirrors the README's Quick Example block at lines 41-63, plus the more elaborate Quick Start at lines 209-234, exercising:
  read_csv_str, query, groupby, agg_named,
  write_json_string, write_feather_bytes,
  Series::from_values, to_datetime,
  write_csv_string, write_sql, read_sql_table, SqlIfExists,
  frankenpandas::rusqlite::Connection::open_in_memory.

Verify with cargo test -p frankenpandas (default features, sql-sqlite enabled). The test must compile only via 'use frankenpandas::prelude::*' to lock in prelude completeness.

If the README example evolves, the test must too — or the README is lying.

Pure additive test. No behavior change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:55:16.374371595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:58:05.930614166Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:58:05.927845775Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-weh1","title":"[LOW] community: no .github/FUNDING.yml — no Sponsor button / funding channels declared","description":"GitHub Sponsors / funding configuration audit.

**Observation.** `ls .github/FUNDING.yml` → No such file. No declared funding channels (GitHub Sponsors / Open Collective / Liberapay / etc.).

**Context.** For an open-source library targeting a sizable user base (pandas has tens of millions of users; frankenpandas targets their Rust-curious subset), a documented funding mechanism is a cheap way to let engaged users contribute financially. GitHub renders a "Sponsor" button on the repo header when `FUNDING.yml` is present; without it, the button is absent and engaged users have no path.

**Severity.** LOW — no operational impact. No release blocker. No code quality issue. Filing truthfully as LOW; user-visible polish, optional feature.

**Fix shape.** `.github/FUNDING.yml`:
```yaml
# Map the repo's "Sponsor" button to one or more funding platforms.
# Each line enables a platform. Remove entries that don't apply.

github: [<org-or-user>]           # GitHub Sponsors
# open_collective: frankenpandas
# liberapay: <name>
# ko_fi: <name>
# custom: ["https://frankenpandas.dev/sponsor"]
```

Requires a decision: does the maintainer have an active GitHub Sponsors account? If yes, one-line file. If not, skip this bead entirely.

Blast radius: 1 tiny file (< 10 lines), zero code changes.

Prerequisites: Maintainer decides which funding channel(s) to expose. Nothing else.

Related:
- br-8k1i (SECURITY.md), br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — same .github/ polish tier.
- No interlocks with other beads.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:18:55.853913193Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:58.138001Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:58.138001Z","close_reason":".github/FUNDING.yml already shipped on main.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["community","funding","review"]}
